intro: Achillea millefolium, also known as yarrow, has been traditionally used to create a poultice to treat wounds, cuts, and bruises. The leaves and flowers of the plant are often crushed and mixed with a binding agent, such as water or oil, to form a paste that can be applied directly to the affected area. The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties of yarrow are believed to help promote healing, reduce pain, and prevent infection.
